role_title: Technical Program Manager, AI Delivery for Public Sector & Defence

location.office_time_required: Ottawa (preferred) or Montreal or Toronto required (proximity to government customers)

secondary_archetype: AI/ML enterprise delivery with government & defence focus

level_signals: 6+ years of experience as a customer-facing Technical or Engineering Program Manager
Compensation range $160,000–$245,000 CAD, consistent with senior individual contributor
Expected to present to senior government officials and departmental executives independently
Serves as technical program lead and trusted advisor for most sensitive government engagements

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience as a customer-facing Technical or Engineering Program Manager in AI/ML consulting, technology transformation, or enterprise software delivery.
Excellent executive communication and stakeholder management.
Experience working with government, defence, or heavily regulated customers (public sector, defence contractors).
Proven track record managing complex, multi-stakeholder technical programs with stringent compliance requirements.
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field; advanced degree or technical certs a plus.
Strong understanding of enterprise software deployment architectures (cloud, on-premises, hybrid, air-gapped)
Comfortable discussing API integrations, model inference, data pipelines, and ML operations with technical stakeholders.
Deep understanding of how government organizations procure, evaluate, and deploy technology
Familiarity with Canadian government requirements: data sovereignty, privacy laws (Privacy Act), accessibility standards, official languages obligations, security classifications
Understanding of defence and national security considerations in AI deployment
Eligible to obtain Canadian government security clearance (Top Secret preferred)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Active Canadian government security clearance (Secret or Top Secret) - strong preference for active Top Secret clearance
Background in or strong familiarity with Canadian defence, intelligence, or critical infrastructure sectors
Experience with GC Cloud services (Azure, AWS GovCloud) or Protected B/C environments
Project management certification (PMP, PgMP) or government-specific methodologies.
Working knowledge of or experience with modern AI: LLMs, agentic systems, MCP, RAG, fine-tuning, evals, and responsible AI would be a strong differentiator
Experience with Algorithmic Impact Assessments or AI ethics frameworks is a strong asset
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field; advanced degree or technical certs a plus.

scope.surface: Canadian public sector and defence AI deployments — end-to-end technical program delivery from initial deployment through production scaling
scope.business_model: B2G (business-to-government), enterprise AI platform and services
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of AI solutions meeting government-grade security and compliance standards
Successful deployment and scaling of AI in government production environments
Customer satisfaction and trust with departmental CIOs, IT directors, and government stakeholders
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end technical program delivery for Canadian public sector and defence customers, from initial deployment through production scaling
Serve as the primary technical program point of contact for government customers
Translate complex government requirements into actionable engineering plans
Navigate government procurement processes, RFP responses, and contractual technical obligations
Create and manage project schedules accounting for government timelines, security review processes, and ministerial approval cycles
Identify and manage dependencies across government departments, security authorities, cloud service providers, and internal Cohere teams
Navigate risks unique to government deployments: privacy compliance, Algorithmic Impact Assessments, Treasury Board submissions, security authorizations
Coordinate technical work across multiple teams: model customization, security hardening, integration with government systems, bilingual support
Problem-solve complex technical challenges in air-gapped, on-premises, or highly regulated environments

green_flags: Highly specialized niche role with clear domain focus (Canadian public sector & defence AI)
Explicit clarity on security clearance requirements and what happens without active clearance (non-classified accounts while clearance is obtained)
Strong benefit package including 6 weeks paid vacation, 100% parental leave top-up, RRSP matching
Encourages applicants who don't meet every qualification
Explicit commitment to inclusive hiring and accommodations
red_flags: Ottawa preferred — strong geographic constraint tied to proximity to government customers; limits candidate pool significantly
Top Secret clearance strongly preferred but 12-18 month wait for candidates without active clearance may create a slow ramp period
No equity mentioned explicitly despite senior scope — unclear if equity is part of total compensation
Currency is CAD, not USD — JD schema base_range_usd cannot be populated accurately without conversion
Highly regulated and security-sensitive environment may limit speed of onboarding and impact

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, comp range, and scope of responsibility)

ambiguities: Security clearance requirement is described as 'strongly preferred' for active Top Secret but also states candidates eligible and willing to obtain clearance will be considered — creating ambiguity about whether clearance is a hard requirement or a strong preference. Treated as hard requirement (eligibility) with active TS as a nice-to-have.
JD does not specify equity or stock options in the compensation section, but the company is a venture-backed AI startup where equity is commonly included — cannot confirm without more information.

Location described as 'Ottawa (preferred) or Montreal or Toronto required' — onsite classification used since proximity to government customers is explicitly required, though exact office attendance policy is not defined.
